claims.evidence_text: The measured seasonal ETc of wheat was ranged from 294.5 to 453.5 mm.
claims.claim_summary: Measured seasonal ETc of wheat 294.5-453.5 mm (drip vs surface)

claims.evidence_text: Estimated crop coefficient values (0.71, 1.31, 1.34 and 0.76 at initial, crop development, mid season and late season stage) at 1.0 Epan through drip were slightly higher over the FAO Kc values.
claims.claim_summary: Kc initial stage 0.71 at 1.0 Epan drip

claims.evidence_text: Estimated crop coefficient values (0.71, 1.31, 1.34 and 0.76 at initial, crop development, mid season and late season stage) at 1.0 Epan through drip were slightly higher over the FAO Kc values.
Estimated crop coefficient values (0.71, 1.31, 1.34 and 0.76 at initial, crop development, mid season and late season stage) at 1.0 Epan through drip were slightly higher over the FAO Kc values.
claims.claim_summary: Kc late season 0.76 at 1.0 Epan drip

claims.evidence_text: until an optimal leaf area index (leaf area index = >4.0) is achieved, that maximizes canopy photosynthesis
claims.claim_summary: Optimal leaf area index >4.0 maximizes canopy photosynthesis
Seed rate 125 kg/ha (LOK-1, drip irrigation experiment)

claims.evidence_text: grain and straw yield of wheat increased significantly at each higher levels of drip irrigation up to 1.0 Epan and fertigation of 120 kg N ha-1 in both the years.
claims.claim_summary: Nitrogen fertigation of 120 kg N/ha gave significant yield increase

claims.evidence_text: it increased continuously during vegetative stage, flowering and grain filling stage (4.75 mm day-1 ) and decreased during the maturity stage.
claims.claim_summary: Peak daily ETc 4.75 mm/day during flowering/grain filling
